description: Puerto Rican criminal organization founded in the 1980s by Quitoni Martinez in Aguada, Puerto Rico. It operates as a mafia-style group with six crime families, involved in drug trafficking, racketeering, extortion, and money laundering. The organization has connections with Colombian cartels and other international crime groups, and maintains a presence in the United States, Mexico, Spain, and Panama.

country_links.evidence_quote: buying $20 million worth of coke from the Colombian cartel
country_links.context: The organization buys cocaine from Colombian cartels and has connections with the Cali Cartel.

country_links.evidence_quote: the mafia was grossing $956 million in revenue from the United States and Japan alone
country_links.context: The organization generates revenue from Japan.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Gulf Cartel soon started blackmailing the Puerto Rican mafia, which resulted in the kidnapping and killing of several members of the Gulf Cartel
country_links.context: The organization has dealings with the Gulf Cartel in Mexico, including blackmail and killings.
The organization has territory in Panama.

country_links.evidence_quote: They even started transporting weapons to Spain and Texas
country_links.context: The organization transports weapons to Spain and has a house there used for a meeting.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: United States (New York, Massachusetts, Florida, Connecticut, Pennsylvania, Illinois, New Jersey)
country_links.context: The organization has territory in multiple U.S. states including New York, Massachusetts, Florida, Connecticut, Pennsylvania, Illinois, and New Jersey.
